medications.indication_rationale: Simple analgesia for any joint discomfort; none currently.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1 g PO up to QID as needed (adult, no contraindication stated)

medications.indication_rationale: Avoid NSAIDs given possible renal involvement in IgA vasculitis until renal function is checked.
Reserved for significant GI or renal involvement and decided after assessment, not via telehealth.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Elevate the legs when resting, and watch urine colour and for abdominal pain; attend the same-day assessment.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces dependent purpura and enables detection of renal/GI complications.

patient_education_points.point: The good news is your rash is very likely IgA vasculitis — inflammation of tiny skin blood vessels after your recent cold — and you're well, which is strongly against the meningitis infection.
But a rash that doesn't fade under a glass still needs a same-day check of your platelets, kidneys and blood pressure, which I can't do over video — please get a blood and urine test today.
Go to ED or call 000 immediately if you develop a fever or feel very unwell, the rash spreads rapidly onto your trunk, you get severe tummy pain or vomiting, or your urine turns red/brown.

follow_up_plan.interval: same-day bloods/urine; GP review in 3-5 days
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Platelet count, urinalysis (haematuria/proteinuria), blood pressure and renal function; monitor for nephritis over weeks.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Fever/toxicity, rapid trunkal spread, severe abdominal pain/vomiting, or cola-coloured urine — ED/000.

must_include_items: Recognise non-blanching purpura needs same-day assessment even in a well patient
Arrange same-day FBC/platelets, urinalysis and blood pressure
Screen for and safety-net meningococcaemia and HSP renal/GI complications
acceptable_alternatives: Same-day GP/urgent care or ED for the bloods/urine
Leg elevation and simple analgesia for comfort
errors_of_omission: Reassuring a non-blanching rash to stay home with no bloods/urine
errors_of_commission: Concluding 'not meningitis, so no assessment needed'
Prescribing NSAIDs with possible renal involvement
Empirical antibiotics in a well afebrile patient
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: High-risk case: passing REQUIRES same-day assessment (platelets/urine/BP) for non-blanching purpura plus meningococcal/renal safety-nets. Remote reassurance to stay home is a failure.
